column1: # This file is the single source of truth for which V-Dem columns are
# read in Stage 2. Every Stage 2 V-Dem adapter call resolves its
# `raw_column` list from this file. Adding/removing an indicator here
# is the only change needed to widen or narrow the V-Dem ingestion.
# Scale conventions in V-Dem v16 (verified against data/raw/vdem/codebook.pdf):
#   - The v2x_* main indices are Interval
#   - The * (no suffix) versions of type-C variables like v2csreprss and
#     v2clkill are the Bayesian Measurement Model point estimates. They
#     are continuous and CAN be negative (the survey scale is 0-4
#     the estimate is on an unbounded latent scale). Example: v2csreprss
#     estimates range roughly -3.72 to +3.18 per the codebook.
#   - The *_ord suffix (e.g. v2clsocgrp_ord) is the ordinalized survey
#   - v2x_mpi and v2x_regime are both interval/categorical:
#       * v2x_mpi is the "Multiplicative polyarchy index" on a 0-1 scale
#       * v2x_regime is the regime classifier (0=closed autocracy
#   - Missing-value sentinel: any value <= -999.0 is treated as missing.
#     This is the V-Dem convention; other negative values on the
#     continuous C-type estimates are LEGITIMATE data
# Direction conventions used by the Stage 5 score modules:
#   - `higher_is_better=1` -- the indicator value maps directly to "more
#   - `higher_is_better=0` -- the indicator must be inverted before
# Rating categories are the 8 from requirement §4. V-Dem does not cover
